_metadata.purpose: Float32 -> float64 migration witness. The `pre_float64_migration` block records the metric values produced by the pre-migration optimizer hot path (FastMapState + MapTopology in float32). The `post_float64_migration` block records the values under the migrated (uniformly float64) implementation. Together they document that the migration preserved metric semantics: shifts are at the ~1e-7 level (float32 precision contract) for FastMapState, and ~1e-11 for spatial_metrics (which was already mostly float64). The parity test (tests/test_metric_parity.py) pins current values against `post_float64_migration` at near-bit-equality (rtol=1e-12, atol=1e-14); a diff against either block beyond ~1e-6 indicates the migration changed metric semantics, not just precision, and is an actionable test failure.
_metadata.regeneration_command_pre: (captured 2026-05-07 at git_hash 6ec5031, before float64 migration; this block is frozen and should not be regenerated)
_metadata.regeneration_command_post: pixi run python scripts/capture_float64_witness.py  (re-capture after any code change touching the metric hot path; if values differ from the post block by more than ~1e-12, investigate before committing)
_metadata.tolerance_policy: Tightening the parity-test tolerance below 1e-12 is permitted only if the post-migration values still hold under the current code. The 1e-6 envelope vs the pre-migration block is the semantic-preservation contract; the 1e-12 envelope vs the post-migration block is the no-regression contract.

delta_vs_pre_float64_migration.interpretation: All deltas within the float32 precision contract (~1e-7 relative for moderate-magnitude values); the migration shifted numerics within precision, not semantics. Critically, post-migration the two impls (FastMapState vs spatial_metrics) now agree to bit-equality on both Moran's I (1.5e-13 absolute, accumulated round-off only) and JFI (0.0 — identical bits).

textbook_path_graph.description: Hand-computed reference: 4-node path 1-2-3-4 with values [1,2,3,4]; I = 0.4 exact
